name: Control One bank baseline OpenZiti service intent
description: Production service and policy templates for a bank-owned OpenZiti deployment connected to Control One imports.
guardrails: Sensitive admin, database, and internal application listeners should be dark services, not public listeners.
Dial identities and bind identities must be separate.
Role attributes should drive service policies rather than one-off permanent identity exceptions.
Break-glass identities require ticket references and time-boxed expiry.
ZAC and controller administration must remain behind a hardened management path.

identity_roles.purpose: Security administrators allowed to dial hardened admin services.
Platform administrators allowed to dial infrastructure admin services.
Database administrators allowed to dial database services.
Application operators allowed to dial application services.
Time-boxed emergency responders with ticket-backed membership.
Router/tunneler bind identities in production networks.

templates.name: Production bind identities
guards: Bind identities belong to routers or tunnelers, not human users.
Enrollment JWTs are short-lived and stored in the approved secret manager.
Control One import shows router health for every production bind location.

templates.name: Administrative dial access
guards: MFA is enforced by the identity provider.
Administrative identities are reviewed at least monthly.
No broad wildcard service role is attached to human admin identities.

templates.name: Database dial access
guards: Database admin membership requires manager and security approval.
Application operator roles cannot dial database service roles.
Control One exposure reconciliation shows no public listener for the same database endpoint.

templates.name: Application dial access
guards: No SSH, RDP, or database service role is included.
Application endpoints are mapped to approved CMDB services.
Control One imports verify that only expected roles can dial.

templates.name: Emergency dark-service break-glass
guards: Ticket id is recorded in identity metadata.
